title: [CI:DOCS] Add SELinux volume information to troubleshoot.md
body:
This issue happens enough that we need to put it on
the top of the troubleshoot guide.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I was able to circumvent the problem with

```
$ chcon -Rt svirt_sandbox_file_t /mnt/home/aanno/scm/scm/docker-compose-files/config/solr
```

see #5796. Perhaps it would be useful to mention that as well.

@aanno That is actually not the preferred way.  The issue with what you did is you set the labeling to be shared amoungst containers.  So if one container broke out , it would be allowed to read/write your content.  If you use the :Z method, it would be private to your container.  chcon container_file_t ... is equivalent to :z.
